concept.conceptName: Calcium.ionized | Blood mixed venous | Chemistry - non-challenge
Calcium.ionized | Serum or Plasma | Chemistry - non-challenge
Calcium.ionized/Calcium.total corrected for albumin in Blood
Calcium.ionized/Calcium.total corrected for albumin in Serum or Plasma

en.name: Ionized calcium in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Ionized (free) calcium is the biologically active fraction of total calcium — typically ~50 % of total calcium in plasma. It is measured directly by ion-selective membrane electrodes on blood gas analysers and on dedicated biochemistry platforms. It is the variable of interest in critical care (hypocalcaemia, transfusion-related hypocalcaemia, post-cardiac-surgery monitoring) where rapid acid-base disturbances or hypoalbuminaemia make total calcium misleading [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or as a mass concentration in mg/dL [2]. This concept set aggregates **ionized calcium** measurements across blood specimens (serum, plasma, arterial, venous, mixed venous, capillary and unspecified blood). Cord blood and fetus specimens are excluded. **Note: ionized vs total calcium.** This concept set covers **ionized (free) calcium** only. Total calcium (including the protein-bound and complexed fractions) is captured by a separate concept set, "Calcium in blood". The two are different measurands with different reference ranges and clinical interpretation; they must not be merged. **Note: actual vs. pH-adjusted ionized calcium.** LOINC distinguishes two forms: - **Ionized calcium** — measured at the patient's actual pH. - **Ionized calcium adjusted to pH 7.4** — recalculated as if pH were 7.4, to neutralise the effect of acid-base status on calcium binding. In the absence of acid-base disturbance the two values are nearly identical, but they can diverge in acidosis or alkalosis. **Both forms are included in this concept set** because most clinical sources and studies aggregate them under "ionized calcium". Study designs that explicitly compare actual vs. pH-adjusted ionized calcium should preserve the distinction at the source value level. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on six LOINC Hierarchy concepts (one per blood specimen, scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), with their descendants pulled in. After exclusions, the set resolves to 22 LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Serum or plasma - *3021347 / 1995-0 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: most widely used substance-concentration form. - *3027694 / 17863-2 — Calcium.ionized [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *3013784 / 12180-6 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Ion-selective membrane electrode (ISE) (LOINC)*: ISE method explicit. - *3024250 / 17864-0 — Calcium.ionized [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Ion-selective membrane electrode (ISE) (LOINC)*: ISE method explicit, mass form. - *3015774 / 13959-2 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma by calculation (LOINC)*: derived by calculation (rather than directly measured). - *3016431 / 19072-8 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] adjusted to pH 7.4 in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: pH-adjusted form. - *40760451 / 57333-7 — Calcium.ionized [Mass/volume] adjusted to pH 7.4 in Serum or Plasma by Ion-selective membrane electrode (ISE) (LOINC)*: pH-adjusted form, mass, ISE. ### Generic blood (specimen unspecified) - *3021119 / 1994-3 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: typically reported by a blood gas analyser. - *3036426 / 38230-9 — Calcium.ionized [Mass/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: same measurand expressed as mass concentration. - *3050068 / 47596-2 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Blood by Ion-selective membrane electrode (ISE) (LOINC)*: ISE method explicit. - *3048816 / 47598-8 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] adjusted to pH 7.4 in Blood (LOINC)*: pH-adjusted form. ### Arterial blood - *3044331 / 34581-9 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood (LOINC)* - *40762532 / 59470-5 — Calcium.ionized [Mass/volume] in Arterial blood (LOINC)* - *3041706 / 53138-4 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] adjusted to pH 7.4 in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: pH-adjusted form. - *40762534 / 59472-1 — Calcium.ionized [Mass/volume] adjusted to pH 7.4 in Arterial blood by Ion-selective membrane electrode (ISE) (LOINC)*: pH-adjusted form, mass, ISE. ### Venous blood - *3033705 / 41645-3 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Venous blood (LOINC)* - *40762533 / 59471-3 — Calcium.ionized [Mass/volume] in Venous blood (LOINC)* - *3041671 / 53140-0 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] adjusted to pH 7.4 in Venous blood (LOINC)*: pH-adjusted form. - *40762535 / 59473-9 — Calcium.ionized [Mass/volume] adjusted to pH 7.4 in Venous blood by Ion-selective membrane electrode (ISE) (LOINC)*: pH-adjusted form, mass, ISE. ### Mixed venous blood - *3032271 / 41646-1 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Mixed venous blood (LOINC)*: typically from a Swan-Ganz catheter. ### Capillary blood - *3035279 / 41644-6 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Capillary blood (LOINC)* - *3039602 / 53139-2 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] adjusted to pH 7.4 in Capillary blood (LOINC)*: pH-adjusted form. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre) for `[Moles/volume]` codes and **`mg/dL`** (concept 8840) for `[Mass/volume]` codes. Conversion: `1 mmol/L ≈ 4.01 mg/dL`. ## Excluded Concepts The hierarchies above pull in two LOINC concepts that are explicitly excluded: ### Ratios — different measurand (ratio to total calcium) - *3032710 / 49936-8 — Calcium.ionized/Calcium.total corrected for albumin in Blood* - *3032782 / 49935-0 — Calcium.ionized/Calcium.total corrected for albumin in Serum or Plasma* The set also deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts: - **Cord blood specimens** (e.g. `40757498 / 54367-8 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Cord blood`, `40757497 / 54366-0 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] adjusted to pH 7.4 in Cord blood`): physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. - **Blood drawn from CRRT circuit** (e.g. via `LP385977-6` hierarchy): specialised intra-circuit monitoring, not a routine patient measurement. - **Body fluid, CSF, dialysis fluid, nonbiological fluid specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "ionized calcium", "iCa", "free calcium", "Ca++" without further specification: `3021347 / 1995-0 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. If the source comes from a blood gas analyser, prefer `3021119 / 1994-3 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Blood` or the arterial variant. - **Source explicitly labels the value as "adjusted to pH 7.4"** (or "pH-corrected ionized calcium"): map to the corresponding `adjusted to pH 7.4` LOINC code for the specimen. Do **not** merge with the actual ionized calcium without checking the study design. - **Source reports "calcium" or "Ca" without specifying ionized/total**: by default, total calcium is the routine biochemistry measurement; map to the "Calcium in blood" concept set unless the source unambiguously labels the result as ionized. - **Do not confuse with**: total calcium (separate concept set), corrected calcium (separate concept set, "Corrected calcium in blood"). ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 1995-0 — Calcium.ionized [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/1995-0/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
